title: Simple Frame That Flips to a Chess Board
summary: A plain picture-frame panel on one side, a hand-taped checkerboard on the other — flip it over and it's a chess board, flip it back and a clipboard clip holds a photo or print.
description:
The frame itself is nothing more than a 1x2 rectangle around a 1/4" plywood panel — build it flat, square, and simple, since the whole design idea lives in how the panel's two faces are finished, not in the frame's joinery. A clipboard clip mounted to the frame edge holds a photo or print on the display side.

**The checkerboard face is painted, not built from inlaid wood** — mask an eight-by-eight grid with painter's tape at least 1-3/4" wide, spray the dark squares black, then paint the light squares white once the black has cured. Pull the tape carefully while the paint is still workable enough not to chip the edges, and seal the finished board with polyurethane so it holds up to real game play.

Pair the finished board with a separate chess set — the board is what this plan builds, not the pieces.

steps.title: Build the frame
steps.body: Assemble the four 1x2 frame sides around the plywood panel, keeping the frame square.

steps.title: Paint the checkerboard face
steps.body: Mask an eight-by-eight grid on the panel with painter's tape, spray the dark squares black, let cure, then paint the light squares white.

steps.title: Seal the board
steps.body: Once both colors have fully cured, apply polyurethane over the checkerboard face for durability.

steps.title: Add the clip
steps.body: Mount the clipboard clip to the frame edge on the display side.

unresolvedImages.alt: Simple picture frame that flips over to a painted chess board
